body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
closes #965


pipeline tested here: https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-coarse-nightly-amip/builds/19
The runs currently fail due to instability, but are being actively debugged

## Content
- [x] add config file for low-res AMIP run
  - copy from config/amip_configs/amip.yml, just change resolution
- [x] add new pipeline
  - use main branches
  - run on clima
  - run 4-6 simulations
- [x] schedule pipeline to run nightly (7pm)
- [x] includes changes from https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/pull/974
title: add nightly coarse amip pipeline

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Change the initial condition of land water to be more consistent with the literature. Before the land is too wet and with this change, the land is much drier and probably too dry. I think it's still a better initial condition. I set it so that the initial W / W_f is 75%, which is the threshold for beta. Hopefully, the initial condition doesn't have too much effect.

Also changes to mono_surface: false in amip ci configs. This is mostly because one of the simulations becomes unstable with mono_surface: true. But it probably makes sense to unify them as all other amip configs have mono_surface: false.

Also changes some parameters of the land to be more consistent with the literature.

## To-do
<!---  list of proposed tasks in the PR, move to "Content" on completion 
- Proposed task
-->


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->

title: change initial condition and parameters for land

title: Include surface elevation in initialization of land surface temp

## Purpose 
closes https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/issues/832

Use `mktempdir` for temporary regridding directories, instead of using `mkpath(path)` and manually calling `rm(path)`

## Content
- [x] use `mktempdir` for the regrid folder created in `setup_output_dirs`
- [x] use `mktempdir` for regrid dirs in `regridder_tests.jl` and `regridder_mpi_tests.jl`
- [x] remove `mkpath(REGRID_DIR)` call in Regridder
